title: Keyword Research Philosophy
content_md:
# Keyword Research Philosophy

- Code: 654574fd
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=654574fd19ee4cb09f9ced8ec19a34b0

transcript.text: All right, so let's talk about keyword research now to be honest keyword research before
ChatGPT before some Google algorithm updates keyword research was a lot more important than it is now
Typically now we don't spend a huge amount of time on it
Again, we have chatGPT to help us and we'll go through those prompts
But arguably even more importantly the prevalence of topical authority
Topical prevalence is becoming more and more important in Google's algorithm
So keyword research is less important than it used to be but it's we still something that we focus on okay, so
keyword research
The first thing that we want to do is
Figure out what keywords we can rank for
Especially if it's a site that's not currently receiving any traffic for non-branded keywords, right? We don't really care about branded keywords
It's easy to rank for branded keywords
So when we're looking at a site we want to focus on how much traffic are they getting from non-branded keywords
And if that number is very small, then we're going to focus on just getting them some traffic from keywords that they can actually rank for
We also want to make sure that we keep in our head that we want to build topical authority by selecting relevant keywords for
Topical authority not always something that we used to do
Sometimes we'll ask our clients if they have any specific keywords in mind
Well, I shouldn't say sometimes we always ask our clients if they have any specific keywords in mind
But they're always almost always going to give us keywords that they don't have any chance at all in ranking for like
they'll have a site that has a domain rating of five and they'll give us a bunch of keywords that they want to rank for and
To rank for any of them. They would need a domain rating of like 80, right?
And they they're not spending millions of dollars. So
Most of the time when clients give us
Suggestions that they're just way too competitive
so what we have to do is to bring them some traffic is
Find keywords that are less competitive and gradually build that authority relevance domain ratings so that we can snowball
Their ranking. Okay. Okay, so this is an important point when we are
Optimizing an article when we're writing an article when we're using page optimizer pro when we're using it as part of our topical relevance
Each URL gets one target keyword, right?
We cannot optimize a URL effectively for more than one keyword
So do not try to optimize a URL for two or three or four keywords you get to choose one
Okay, and I know that can be hard, but that's it. You just choose one keyword for one URL
So when we're looking at keyword research, we clearly want to find in an ideal world
High volume low competition keywords now volume and competition are obviously both relevant to your client
I have a client who currently gets 20 or 30 clicks a month from Google search console
Which they're incredibly happy about because each client is worth 50 to $60,000 form
So they're in a very very small search volume niche 50 or 60 clicks a month is huge
And then I have other clients who you know
10,000 clicks a month is a round off error and
This is important and we'll talk about how to write AI content effectively
But the important thing to keep in mind is that it's inexpensive
To write AI content content used to be expensive
So we would focus a lot on prioritizing which keywords are actually trying to rank for but now content is very inexpensive
So generally, we're just going to do all of them. Okay, there's no reason to like, you know
Stress out over which ones we should go for first. We'll just do all of them content is cheap
I will say yes, we're going to choose one keyword and optimize for it
But we're going to
Rank for a variety of similar keywords, even if you've only optimized for one
So the clear example of that of course personal injury lawyer and personal injury attorney
You'll generally find those two terms have almost the same search results
And if you rank for one, you'll rank for the other
So what we need to do is identify these similar queries
Otherwise our keyword search volume is going to be way off to go back to this personal injury lawyer and personal injury attorney
Personal the search volume for those two
We should be adding them when we're looking at that keyword because we know we're going to rank for both of them
If we rank for one of them, so we're going to optimize the one that has the higher search volume
We're almost always going to optimize around the one that has the higher search volume
But we know that will probably rank for both of them
So we want to add the search volume together
To correctly identify how much search volume we're targeting with that keyword
Okay, and any time we want to see if Google sees two different keywords as similar
Just type them into Google if the search results are very very similar then we know Google sees them as similar
So we add all these search volumes up of these very similar keywords
Then we do a weighted average of the difficulty if we didn't do that then we would end up with
Lower estimated volumes that we're actually going for but again
This used to be something we would spend four or five hours on we don't anymore content is so inexpensive
Really when we're looking at keyword research, we just want to get a broad
broad
ideas for keywords with some AI prompts and
Leverage that along with topical relevance to find the content targets. We're going to write about
